pattern: Since exactly one element must always be removed, a sliding window that tolerates at most a single zero turns 'delete one element, keep the longest run of ones' into 'find the widest window with <= 1 zero, then drop one slot for the deletion.'
hints: You must delete exactly one element no matter what, even if the array is already all ones -- so think of the answer as one less than the widest stretch you can make workable.
Slide a window across the array that is allowed to contain at most one zero; whenever it would need a second zero, shrink from the left until it's valid again.

At every window position the deletion budget is spent on the (at most one) zero inside it -- or, if there is none, on any one of the ones -- so the answer is simply the window width minus one, tracked as the width shrinks and grows.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, delete the single zero
statement example, longer array with two zeros
statement example, all ones, must still delete one
single element that is a zero
single element that is a one
all zeros, no ones exist at all
two adjacent zeros, only one can be tolerated
zeros at both ends bracketing a long run
alternating pattern with no long runs

stress.description: large random binary array with a controlled zero density
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    zero_prob = rng.choice([0.02, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5])
    nums = [0 if rng.random() < zero_prob else 1 for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
